source: EIA — World Oil Transit Chokepoints, Hormuz flows by destination (2024 baseline). Volumes sum to total Hormuz throughput — the validator enforces it. Strategic-stock figures per row carry their own source and status; China's are state-opaque and marked estimate.
notes: Model: an importer's loss at disruption d = hormuz_mbd × d × (stranded/gross) — bypass relief is distributed pro-rata across buyers because rerouted barrels are fungible (Petroline crude landing at Yanbu still reaches its buyer). days_cover = stocks ÷ loss.

importers.name: China
importers.stocks_source: Public analyst estimates — government SPR only; state-opaque, satellite-derived ranges 280–1,000 incl. commercial

importers.name: India
importers.stocks_source: ISPRL phase-I caverns (5.33 MMT ≈ 39 M bbl); phase-II expansion pending

importers.name: Japan
importers.stocks_source: METI national + private obligated stocks (~230 days of net imports, IEA)

importers.name: Other Asia & Pacific
importers.stocks_source: Aggregate of smaller strategic programs (Thailand, Taiwan, etc.)

importers.name: Europe (OECD)
importers.stocks_source: IEA-member obligated public + industry stocks attributable to Europe
